summary: Well-structured shared_meta blueprint defining the process-blueprint lifecycle: discovery, session assignment, and expectation-driven idle watchdog behavior. Locally self-describing with clear artifact/actor/policy decomposition and meaningful causal chains. Moderate uncertainty on live integration since no companion JSON contracts or referencing files are visible in provided context.
strengths: Role statement immediately communicates purpose: machine-readable execution contracts for session-scoped watchdog behavior
Clean artifact/actor/policy decomposition keeps concerns separated without over-fragmenting
defines() block eliminates interpretive slack on every key term including the subtle JsonPrimaryContract vs OptionalAgentishCompanion distinction
when() chains encode specific operational triggers (idle transition, assignment, pairing) rather than generic platitudes
Abstraction stays consistently at the meta level without leaking into JSON schema fields or implementation paths
weaknesses: No live-use evidence visible: no companion ProcessBlueprintJson files or system code referencing these concepts appear in provided context
defines() entries for straightforward concepts (SharedBlueprintCatalogPresence, SessionScopedExpectation) restate what the concept name already conveys, adding tokens without new semantic payload
The file does not hint at the expected JSON schema shape for ProcessBlueprintJson, leaving a gap between the meta contract and any implementer trying to create a conforming artifact
